---
title: "Talking with LLMs via code"
subtitle: "Programming with LLMs for Data Practitioners"
author: "Julia Silge | Posit PBC"
footer: "<https://juliasilge.github.io/applied-stats-byu-2026/>"
highlight-style: atom-one
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    transition: fade
    preview-links: auto
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-image: "../images/undraw_chat-with-ai_ir62.svg"
      data-background-size: "auto 50%"
      data-background-position: "82% 85%"
      data-background-repeat: "no-repeat"
---

# Hello!

<center>

<img src="https://github.com/juliasilge.png" style="border-radius: 50%;" width="300px"/>

[{{< fa brands github >}} \@juliasilge](https://github.com/juliasilge)

[{{< fa brands bluesky >}} \@juliasilge.com](https://bsky.app/profile/juliasilge.com)

[{{< fa link >}} juliasilge.com](https://juliasilge.com/)

</center>

## Who are you?

-   You know some R and/or Python
-   You are curious about working with LLMs from code
-   You want hands-on practice, not just slides

## Day 1: Build on AI

1.  Talking with LLMs via code (this session)
2.  Programming with LLMs
3.  Augmented generation
4.  Beyond tools

# Welcome and getting started {background-image="../images/undraw_chat-with-ai_ir62.svg" background-size="auto 50%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}
## Welcome (10 min)

-   Introductions
-   What to expect from these two days
-   **Activity:** introduce yourself to your neighbors

## Set-up and verify API access (10 min)

-   How API keys work
-   Where to put them so the workshop code can find them
-   **Activity** (`01-hello-llm`): make your first API call by writing a short "I'm at SIAS 2026" social media post

## Think empirically, be pragmatic (10 min)

-   The right mindset for working with LLMs
-   Models are not deterministic; try things, iterate, compare
-   Extra time here to troubleshoot setup as needed

# Anatomy of a conversation {background-image="../images/undraw_chat-with-ai_ir62.svg" background-size="auto 50%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}
## To get a response, you send a message (20 min)

-   Under the hood: an HTTP request to a provider
-   Message roles: `system`, `user`, `assistant`
-   `ellmer` and `chatlas` hide the boilerplate

## Activity: word guessing game (`02-word-game`)

-   System prompt: *You are playing a word guessing game. At each turn, guess the word and tell us what it is.*
-   First message includes a modifier (e.g. "In British English," "In pirate,") that steers later answers

## The conversation is stateless

-   The model has no memory between requests
-   The library replays history each turn
-   Demo (`clearbot`): show what happens when you clear the chat

# How LLMs work {background-image="../images/undraw_chat-with-ai_ir62.svg" background-size="auto 50%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}
## Tokens as the fundamental unit (20 min)

-   What is a token?
-   How models predict the next token
-   Why this matters for cost and context length
-   Demo: token possibilities visualizer

# Shinychat basics {background-image="../images/undraw_chat-with-ai_ir62.svg" background-size="auto 50%" background-position="82% 85%" background-repeat="no-repeat"}
## Try a chat UI in seconds (20 min)

-   `live_console()` / `live_browser()` in ellmer
-   `chat.console()` / `chat.app()` in chatlas
-   **Activity** (`03-live`): launch a live chat against your own chat object

## Build your own Shiny chat app

-   Shiny `chat_ui()` and `chat_append()` (R: also `chat_mod_server()`)
-   **Activity** (`04-word-game-reverse`): flip the word game so the LLM holds a secret word and gives hints; the user has to guess


# Thanks!

<center>

<img src="https://github.com/juliasilge.png" style="border-radius: 50%;" width="300px"/>

[{{< fa brands github >}} \@juliasilge](https://github.com/juliasilge)

[{{< fa brands bluesky >}} \@juliasilge.com](https://bsky.app/profile/juliasilge.com)

[{{< fa link >}} juliasilge.com](https://juliasilge.com/)

</center>

::: footer
Illustrations from [unDraw](https://undraw.co/) 
:::
